frameworks.name: synaptic facilitation theory of working memory
frameworks.one_line: calcium-mediated short-term synaptic facilitation in recurrent cortex stores items

frameworks.name: persistent reverberatory spiking
frameworks.one_line: standard view that sustained spiking activity is the memory substrate

frameworks.name: recurrent neocortical network model
frameworks.one_line: recurrent cortical networks extended with presynaptic calcium buffer dynamics

core_question: What neural substrate sustains working memory if not persistent reverberatory spiking activity?
key_assumptions: presynaptic residual calcium acts as a short-term memory buffer
calcium kinetics have long time constants relative to spiking
recurrent cortical connections support facilitation-based storage
manipulations: refresh rate of spiking activity loading the calcium buffer
comparison of facilitation-based vs persistent-spiking mechanisms
key_findings: calcium facilitation can sustain working memory with sparse refresh spikes
low refresh rate yields metabolically efficient and robust storage
spontaneous activity modulates duration and stability of memory
interpretations: working memory is carried by synaptic facilitation, not persistent spiking
spontaneous activity is a control knob for memory duration and stability
